_comment: Add the 'hooks' block below to your ~/.claude/settings.json. The install script does this automatically.

hooks.prompt: A new Claude Code session has just started. Before responding to the user's first message, silently act as the session-opener agent: (1) read the memory index at ~/.claude/projects/[current-project-slug]/memory/MEMORY.md and any files it references, (2) read docs/next-session.md if it exists in the current project, (3) read the '## Current State' section and the most recent 2 entries in '## Session History' from CLAUDE.md if it exists. The project slug is the cwd path with slashes replaced by dashes and colons removed — e.g. C:\Dev\flex-planner becomes C--Dev-flex-planner. Then deliver a brief status update to the user covering: current phase, last session summary, top 3 next items, and any blockers. If the user's first message is a greeting or question about project status, lead with this brief. If no context files are found, say so honestly rather than pretending there is nothing to report.
